title: Wow oh wow! Phone quality is great too!
text:
[[VIDEOID:b7b8180a2e6b2912cb47489991fd619c]] Running: it was really incredible to listen to an audiobook AND hear the birds and the cars around me. Before using an open ear headphone - I had to choose between entertainment or situational awareness.

Swimming: the aeropex headphones worked great when I was treading water. The sound quality was superb and consistent. Swimming laps on the other hand - it tended to cut out. I’m curious if it was a Bluetooth connection issue OR if it was when the charging port got wet.

Phone quality: the phone quality was fantastic! I didn’t have to talk as loud - as compare to when using Apple earbuds or my AirPods.

The aeropex headphones stayed in place really well for running, swimming, and even dancing around the house. The deciding factor for me between these and the XTrainers was 1) the price difference 2) I have the option to download music to my fitness watch (which I’ve never loaded music onto until now - for this).

title: $20 sound quality
text:
I bought these Aftershokz in search of a great sound quality sport earphone that doesn’t fall out of my ears, willing to pay good money for the set that satisfies all needs and durable. I have usually done well with the jammer style but some of those have been falling out of my ears. This is my first time with Sftershokz or Bone resonance technology.
The physical design of these are top notch feels like nothing at all, unless you’re going to lay your head back on something. And then I have to acknowledge that it is state of the art technology, the bone resonance, 2000 Tom Clancy stuff.
Like other reviews, the lack of bass is a draw back... I don’t think I hear ANY base. I can feel some lower frequencies vibrating on the contact point and that’s it. I tried the ear plugs and that didn’t help. If i put my fingers all the way in my ear canals I can hear the bass and then there’s other frequencies that sound muffled. I would say the sound totally lacks good frequency range, vibra

title: Exactly like the aeropex, but for smaller heads.
text:
This is a smaller version of the aeropex, which is an excellent headset for every day use.

If you have a smaller head, (distance around the back of your head is 9 1/2" or less) then these would be a good option to try.

They obviously hug tighter than the original aeropex, but this minimizes interactions between the headband and things like collared shirts, or anything that you might lay your head against.

I absolutely recommend the aeropex in general, especially for listening to spoken word content, like podcasts while going about daily life. They're also great for teleconferencing and phone calls.

They don't sound the absolute best compared to conventional headphones, but what you give up in sound quality is more than made up for in usability and convenience.

The headlining feature for bone conduction is the ability to listen to music as well as your environment at the same time, but they also work with earplugs (which are include

title: I can't say enough Good about these!
text: *Edit* I love these things more and more each day. I utterly forget I'm wearing them and I frequently don't charge them at night. Despite this I've noticed I can get at least a full 2 days worth of battery out of these. Let me be clear. I put them on at 9am in the morning before I shower and I don't take them off till about 1am at night. During that 16 hours they are connected in multipoint paring mode to my cell phone and my Nintendo Switch at all times. Of that 16 hours they are on I listen to music about 3 hours of the day and watch probably an hours worth of videos on my phone. I use them for notifications and phone calls mostly allowing me to be hands free and without bothering those around me. I answer probably 5 calls a day and talk on the phone about an hour a day and then in the evenings I listen to audio books for about 5 hours.  So they are on for 16 hours connected to Bluetooth per day every day with about 10 hours of use and about 6 hours of using them solely for notificat

title: Great for background noise while working, but don't expect much more
text:
I've been using these for about 160 hours per month since I got them a few months ago. Initially I was very impressed by the novelty of bone-conduction audio, but now that I'm used to them I can provide an honest review.

I bought these using a company stipend. The reason I chose these over conventional headphones or earbuds was because I have to be able to hear my surroundings at work. I'd like to keep this review concise for your sanity and mine, so here are some things I like and don't like about these headphones.

The good:
• Reasonably lightweight
• Astounding battery life. These last me an entire 12 hour shift without a charge, after months of use
• Acceptable microphone quality for meetings/phone calls
• Good voice clarity in calls or listening to podcasts
• Fairly simple, easy to find controls
• Low profile. You'll sometimes forget you're wearing them and they don't stick out much (I can wear a half face respirator + safety gogg

title: Great! For What They Are
text: These are not the highest quality audio you will ever hear, but they are not engineered for the audiophile. What they do do they do well.  First, sound quality is good, especially for something not in or over your ears — its actually pretty cool when you think about it. They do leave your ears open but the sound does tend to drown out the other sounds, but you have the volume control so adjust accordingly — on high you will hear very little else, on low you can listen and have a conversation.  They do vibrate on your ears with high bass levels, but nothing you won’t get used to if you have mastery of your id. What these do really really well is allow you to comfortably listen for long periods of time without headphone fatigue; I wear them for hours with no discomfort, including when not using them, and while wearing glasses — they are, bar none, the most comfortable headphones I’ve every used. I mainly bought them to listen to audiobooks but find myself using them to enjoy sound when o

title: LOUD BEEPS!!!!
text:
I'm taking off a couple stars because I'll never be able to use the on board buttons for skip and volume control (without removing the speakers from close to my ears first).

They put in STUPID G*D D*MN LOUD beeps on every function, when they could have had no beeps at all. Why do you need to hear a beep at 100% volume to confirm that you've hit the volume down button?

The beeps cause me physical pain, and create sensitivity which lasts for minutes. These headphones already sound very crisp and sharp, and the aftereffects of the beeps make me too sensitive to the sharp sound quality of my media.

The sad thing is that this hardware has no capacity to connect to a computer for data, so there will be no chance to fix this error with a firmware update. This is really unforgivably stupid. I don't know how this made it through the development and testing process without being caught.

title: Novelty headphones
text: These headphones do not have noise cancellation. They come with ear plugs... The sound quality is that of talking with your friend through tin cans and a string. Would not recommend for any activity.

title: Promising but disapponting
text:
Update #2: I got an RMA and exchanged the pair that didn’t work with some new ones….and the same thing happened to the new ones after a couple of weeks. Buy something else if you’re looking for reliability.

Update: Battery completely died after two months of use. The LED won't turn on and I can't even charge them anymore.

I really love these. I've always wanted something that I can wear at work and still be able to hear the cars driving around me and in case someone is trying to talk to me. AirPods have a transparency feature but it just doesn't work well enough for me so these headphones are almost perfect for me. The build quality feels great, they charge fast and easy and the sound quality is very clear for bone conduction (if you don't turn the volume up too high). The only reason I'm taking off two stars is because I keep accidentally calling people because of this stupid auto redial feature they baked into the headphones. I really wish I could turn off this

title: Loses connection constantly and shuts off for no reason
text: My headphones keep randomly disconnecting and shutting off when I still have 60% + battery life. It has happened multiple times a day for the past week or so that I have owned them while listening to audio books or on phone calls. My phone (brand new pixel 5) is in my pocket or within the same room and should not be having connection issues. This is very disheartening, as I had my previous pair of aftershocks for over 4 years and loved them. I plugged them in last night and this morning I can't even get them to turn on. I will sadly be making arrangements to return. The headphones themselves, when they are working, are most excellent and I love the smaller design vs. the old.

title: Great, except for one unforgivable flaw.
text:
Both of the Trekz I got from. Aftershokz have been great I use them so much I need two pairs to recharge one while I use the other. They last me over 8 hours but I use them constantly. One finally had too much abuse and I so I replaced it with this model.

It's really good. An improvement all around, and I don't even really mind the specialized charger due to the water resistant design.

But the beep from on/off or volume adjustments.

The beep. The BEEEP!!!!

It is painfully loud with no remedy on my Samsung Note. I often have to remove it to use any of the buttons. This is a very, very serious and stupidly easy flaw to avoid for an otherwise perfect headset.

Disable the beep. Entirely. I don't need any beep to let me know I pressed a button Turn it off.

Disable. The. Beep.

Update Nov 13, 2021:

I bumped this up to 4 stars due to the way a replacement was handled. The battery stopped accepting a charge

title: So much better than the Air!
text: I keep needing to feel above my ears to check they're still on! They're so light! Keeps my sanity since I can listen to music when my new born twins are sleeping!

title: Love the concept, but this model is defective and should have been recalled
text:
I love the Aftershokz series. After my Aftershokz Airs were damaged by rain, I bought the Aeropek model. Love them too. Lighter, microphone works fine, but they sound clearer to me.

Update: while I love the concept of these, they are not made well and begin to fail after several short months. The internal mechanism on both sides started to rattle and then the battery charge wouldnt last more then 2 minutes before the battery warning came on. It’s a shame because I’m a aftershokz lover and had their previous series the Titanium model, which I loved.
After reading other horror stories I see that this model is plagued with a defect.
I guess it’s time to look for another brand.

title: Looks nice there it ends.
text: Look nice and feels nice when wearing. There the good ends. Cannot turn volume up past 75% then overwhelms the speakers. Not real bone conduction. My first pair lasted about 1 month. Would not charge after that. Now 1 month in on my 2nd pair and per on switch is bad. Amazon will not w as warranty. Hmmmm

title: Won't charge after less than 10 months
text: One star since it will not charge after less than a year???

title: Perfect if you’ve ever had trouble with traditional in-ear headphones!
text: These have absolutely fantastic!  I have always had issues with ear pieces not fitting in my small ears, and I cannot wear bulky noise-cancelling headphones at work. These are perfect! Fit great (although I wish the behind the neck bit could be adjusted,) they sound fantastic, I can still hear my surroundings (a necessity) and battery life is beyond expectations.

title: These are AMAZING!
text: I LOVE my AfterShokz Aeropex.  They are better than I expected.  Design is comfortable to wear all day.  Stays in places.  Sounds great!  If you are in the market for great sounding headphones that are awesome for music and great for conversations than you must get a pair.

title: Awesome & Safest Ear Phones Yet
text:
Positive: The best ear phones I've experienced yet. Other designs give me ear infections. Also, much easier to hear what's going on around you, yet produces a clear, sound quality.
Negative: I do get a headache with extended use:
Recommend limited time

title: Terrible
text:
If you like to hear the singer or all the instruments in your music don’t buy these.

Imagine a regular pair of ear buds on loud and you hold them about a foot from your ears. THATS WHAT THESE SOUND LIKE. It’s frustrating. Lol

The one good thing about them is they stay put. You can run, jump, drive a motorcycle anything. They stay in place. But you can’t hear anything.

title: I love these headphones...  Fabulous functionality...
text: Want keep awareness of whatever is around you?  Want your headset to stay in place while moving?  This has good sound, but it is not  an audiophile headphone.  Working outside, on a bike, walking around the house and want to hear your kids or wife?  This is the device.  Nothing else like it...  It is supposed to be water resistant...  ???  If I believed it, I would use it in the shower.  Strongly recommend...  I bought a pair for my grandson.  His parent bought their own after seeing him use it while on his bike, etc..

title: New set - New review
text:
The verdict is still out as I’ve only had these a couple of days but here’s my thoughts:
*They're improved over the previous versions in that they don’t vibrate as much—still there’s some with bass.
*the sound is better but still definitely not great

*they're more comfortable but still lacking in snugness and custom fit. You can’t adjust them.
*the design idea is great but does not deliver a superior sound—it’s only acceptable

Bottom line—they’re still way overpriced and they need a lot of improvements before they’ll be considered great. So yah they’re over priced!! We want a custom, snug fit!! These are not that. So I’m not sure I’ll keep them due to the over pricing.

Now it’s January and they will not maintain a charge longer than 30 minutes with very little use. TERRIBLE for the price. I am very sorry I bought them!!

I just purchased the updated version because I love the way they fit without putting something in my ears. I

title: Impulse purchase that I decided to keep
text:
I impulsively purchased these because I was curious about the technology after stopping by my local bike shop. I thought I may end up returning these before I even received them because I own AirPod Pros. Wow, you're not going to get crazy sound fidelity but they work great. You get descent sound quality while still being able to be aware of your surroundings. They are also much more comfortable for me than in-ear and over the ear headphones. There's nothing like these out there. I recommend them for anyone who needs music while still being engaged with their environment. I usually have issues with wrap around headphones coming out of my ears while running, etc... not an issue with these. They have plenty of tension to stay on your head while still being surprisingly comfortable.

I took the Aftershokz on a 30 mile bike ride with a group yesterday. I had descent music while still being able to easily listen for cars and speak with people in my riding group.

The sou

title: Expectations Surpassed
text: For years, I've been buying $30 blue tooth headphones.  Unfortunately, they never worked very long.  I finally decided to spend the money on a better pair of bluetooth headphones.  I've had them for over one month now and I absolutely love them.  For running and the gym, i can hear my music perfectly, but can still hear everything around me.  They're super comfortable and stay firmly in place.  During car and plane trips, I've inserted ear plugs and can hear everything much louder and a low/medium volume (without those around me hearing anything).  I'm super satisfied with AfterShokz Aeropex and would buy them again!

title: It stopped charging. Starting warranty claim now. Return window closed  10 days ago...
text: 10 days after return window closed it turned into a lemon. It won't charge. Wish me luck with the warranty claim!

title: Great concept, painful beeps and pops
text:
Update: dropping from 3 stars to 1 star. 6 months after purchase, battery life has dropped to under 2 hours per charge with 3-4x/wk usage. Again, unacceptable for a premium priced product with modern battery technology.

Original: This is my first set of bone conduction headphones. I bought these to be able to hear my wife, kids, and surroundings while listening to stuff. They work great for that. I love the open ear concept. The physical design is really attractive, but it has very irritating operational issues.
As other reviews have mentioned, the beep is loud, and always at the same volume level. If you put pressure on the sound piece, and press it to your head, it becomes unbearably loud. In addition, there are random, painful, vertigo-inducing pops occasionally, usually when connecting, playing/ pausing, incoming call while playing something, powering on or off, or opening an app. Not all or even most of the time, just every once in awhile. Probably not Aeropex's fa

title: Don't expect warranty support anymore August 2022
text: I have purchased four of these headsets in total, two directly from the manufacturer. I have had issues with all of them. Recently my Aeropex set started acting as if the activation button was stuck pressed in. It would just cycle on, battery high, off, and so on. After trying to get covered warranty support they are completely unresponsive. No phones answered, and replies to emails. Although I got any RMA online, there was no shipping label sent. I found what is supposed to be their address and shipped back the defective headset but still no response after one month. Buyer beware!

title: Amazing!!!!!!!
text: These are amazing!! I really liked the Air... The APEC is even better.  The vibration when volume is high is almost gone.  Sound is louder and clear.  Even lighter than the Air, yet doesn't feel flimsy.  Easy to use. Good battery life.  Wouldn't buy anything but the Aftershokz.  Both Air and Apex are great earphones... If you have the money... Get the Apex!!!

title: This is an amazing product, I’m never taking them off.
text: This is an amazing product with excellent sound quality. They’re very comfortable to wear all day long. You can ride your bike in them with music, or work in a hospital where you have to talk on the phone frequently and you need your hands to be free.  It doesn’t drown out the sounds of barking dogs, oncoming cars, crickets farting, there’s no noise cancellation involved, but it’s very clear sound and you can hear your surroundings, keeping you safe and not antisocial because you ear hole is wide open baby!  I’m never taking these off.  They bass massages your temples.

title: These are great !
text: I wear these at work all day,and pretty much forget they are even on,very comfortable, sound great,veryeasy to operate.the black sets need a logo or something on the buds to make them not look like hearing aids so much.still very happy😀👍👌

title: Love these
text:
Super cool headphones! Great sound imo. The vibrations from bass or loud noises takes a little time to get used too but it’s not bothersome. Calls are clear and I’m clear to who I’m talking too. Super light weight. I forget I’m wearing them. Are you going to get Bose quality sound? No, but they are just as good if not better (I vote better) than your apple pods or wired apple earphones. The noise canceling is strange.. because it does and it doesn’t at the same time. I do have to stop them to hear clearly what someone is saying to me if I’m listening to something. So in a weird way it does cancel sound. But it also doesn’t. It’s hard to explain. Battery lasts forever. Charges quickly. I don’t think I can ever go back to in ear headphones again. *chefs kiss*

(Also, if the company is reading this, the packaging was beautiful all the way around, from imaging, to materials, to how it opens.)

title: If this is what you need, it amazing while it lasts.
text:
I have had aftershokz headphones for the last 4 years and have used them almost daily First I had an air which I purchased shortly after release. I ended up getting another set on sale but gave it away to a friend. That first set of airs I still have and they still work, but battery life has faded predictably. I have used them in the shower, cycling on my commute to work, doing chores around the house. Probably in the 1-3 hours per day. 2 years ago I upgraded to the aeropex and the difference is marked.

1) The sound quality is excellent for open ear headphones. Read some reviews of the sound to understand what you are getting. Base is not a forte here.
2) Voices are clear, call quality is excellent and the microphone is a real standout feature.
3) The fit is excellent and light and it is very easy to forget you are wearing them. Far more comfortable than wearing a mask.
4) The bluetooth range is among the best I have ever had. It can actually be annoying becau

title: They last forever!
text: This technology saved me a lot of pain and hassle, I suffer from swimmers ear or should I say I used to suffer ;-) This things are build to last! People can hear you great, the sound in a loud place is not that great though, yet for me that is not a big deal Since I like to be aware of my surroundings.

title: A Great Upgrade From My Titaniums
text: I have used Aftershokz Titaniums for a couple of years when I am mountain biking. The Aeropex has better sound and I love the magnetic charger. Sound is still not symphony quality but I just listen to podcasts anyway. The over the ear piece is a bit smaller so the Aeropex is a bit more comfortable for long rides. Both of them leave my ears open so I can hear mountain lions sneaking up on me.

title: Great Bone Conduction Headset with a Few Caveats
text:
I love these. I've owned a few different bone conduction (BC) headsets in the past, with my previous favorites being several pairs of AfterShokz Trekz Air. It was the sound quality that I enjoyed the most, considering they were BC, though their detractions were the lack of a back/previous function and was sometimes a bit more prone to interference than I would have liked — but not game breaking.

The Aeropex have mostly exceeded my expectations largely due to the Pros listed below and the reduced Thanksgiving pricing, as I feel the normal pricing of $150+ is higher than I'm willing to spend considering what I've experienced now that this is my second Aeropex purchase (more on that below).

I say mostly exceeded because there are a few things that still don't quite knock it out of the park for me.

Pros: Very comfortable, waterproof, attractive minimalist design, great battery life, and a so-so back/previous function (having it is better than not, more on t

title: For out of ear head phones are great and they stay on, as well as having great sound
text:
So far so good. Having earbuds in my ears has created a problem, so having these out of ear is great, plus I don't get sweaty ears like other out of ear headphones give.

For the most part they stay put. I have a larger sized head and they work fine as long as I am not turning my head too much.

Bass sounds suffer a bit, but the majority of music sound is great.

title: Disappointed
text: I like the sound quality of the previous model (Trekz Air) much better--treble and bass are not as rich on the Aeropex. ) The Aeropex is fine if you've never heard the sound quality of the Trekz Air.  It seems like I may be the only reviewer so far who feels this way. But the truth is we're all different and we all hear things a little differently. You may find that the Aeropex works better for you.

title: For women or smaller dudes
text:
These things fit like a glove. I’m a truck driver and I’m always listening to a podcast of some sort. After hours of the traditional in ear bluetooth my ears would hurt at the end of the day. With these.. I forget they are there honestly. I tried the pro, sound quality is better but after a day of wear of those I realized they are a little too big for me and left a few sore spots in my ears. I wish the pro sound quality was on the mini but it works for what it is. If you are one who loves loud music to drown your thoughts and block out noise these aren’t for you. The thing that confuses me is these are designed so you can hear around you and listen or talk but they send ear plugs. I know the sound quality is better but for me it defeats the purpose.

I would recommend the mini for women since we have smaller heads generally.

I’ve tested out how loud it can be before people can hear what you’re listening to and I can have it all the way up and unless I tell them to

title: It is very very uncomfortable!
text:
I have Airpod,Bose,LG… Now I got this one. I hope it is good for workouts but “No!”. It”s nothing special.

It is very very uncomfortable! Painful product. It”s low volume, constant tingling and vibration sensation on cheek bones .

I will return it if I have time to do . Please read my honest review. I am real Amz customer for more than 15 years.

title: OK product
text: This was sent back to get a new one as the first set didn't work correctly. Received the new set and no one could hear me.

title: Much improved over previous model -- I love 'em
text: My puppies ate my original Aftershokz Titanium headphones, and I upgraded to these to replace them. They are lighter, have better sound quality, a more pleasant interactive voice, and feel more comfortable on my head. I use them each and every day and they recharge nicely and work beautifully. Now, if I can just keep them away from the puppies....

title: Really impressed
text:
I've been running with the older Titanium model for years now without any real drama. Didn't see any real reason to pay for the Air model but now we're two generations ahead and I'm training for a marathon and this seemed like a good time to upgrade.

I have to say the incremental changes added up. They're noticeably lighter, the audio is definitely improved. In theory they're more waterproof, and that makes sense with the new charger, but I run pretty regularly in storms with the old ones with no problems so I'm not sure what prompted that.

Too early to say how these will hold up - my titaniums did break at once point but they replaced them immediately so I'm not super concerned, but they're so thin hopefully they've thought all that through.

title: An Audiophile Review
text:
The packaging deserves mention...beyond anything I have ever bought. Superb! I unpacked the headphones very carefully as I thought there was a real probability that I would return these headphones. I am keeping them.

The headphones will change the sound quality depending on the exact position you have them on, so you can move them around a bit to get the sweet spot for you. The comfort like others have said, is amazing....I too forgot I was wearing them at times.

The sound will also depend on the volume you set them at, for those of you familiar with the Fletcher-Munson Curve. The midrange is STUNNING. Perhaps as clear as it gets. The detail is far beyond my expectations. For you base heads out there, you will be disappointed. Compared to a very good pair of headphones, it lacks dearly. There is severe roll-off of frequencies below 500 kHz. That being said, these headphones give me a good listening experience to classical, and most music that is not bas

title: Long battery life
text: Good headphones. If you like wearing your hair down you'll get tangled up in them. I especially like that no one can tell if I'm on the phone or not. So in public when I see Betsy barreling towards me at the Walmart I can pretend I'm on the phone and keep it pushing!

title: Love these!
text: Great!!  Haven't used them on the trail or treadmill but so far for calls they work great.

title: I've had them for 4 months.
text: Their excellent Bluetooth Devices. So far the best I've ever had.  But at the 4 month mark, they just quit working. I work outside and I go to the gym so I sweat and everything else but they just quit.  I'm contacting support to see what they can do they can do because I really like them but I am very disappointed that they just shut down like that period. So, if you're considering buying them I'll update my post and let you know if you should.  Amazon is been excellent with respect to the issue but the company thatis the company that sells them ignored my request.

title: Great bone conduction earphones
text: Lightweight and with excellent battery life, these are the best bone conduction earphones I have seen to date.  I tend to use the Aeropex all the time, even when I might otherwise use a straight in-ear headset. Sound quality is good (but not exactly the same as traditional earphones).  I prefer having my hearing unimpaired when I am listening to music so these are perfect for me.

title: No more sweaty ear canals!
text: All said and done I am enjoying these headphones/buds very much. In the ear buds no matter how good a fit become irritating to the ear after couple hours. I can wear these as long as I want with no effects. Bass not wonderful but also not non-existent a fair trade off for comfy ears in my opinion. People do look twice and think it's a medical attachment lol...screw em! They work with my glasses on too guess ear piece on glasses would matter if there real thick, I just like these that's all. Very happy

title: Love these!
text: I was very pleased with my headphones/earbuds. I can’t tolerate buds in my ears so decided to try these. Great sound quality as a listening device and my husband says I sound fine on his end. So great product. Glad I bought this device!

title: Do not use this buyer!!!
text: Only buy from aftershock! Do not use this buy!!! They sent me a defective set the first time and a used set the second!!!

title: Can you hear me now. Yep!
text: My son love's them. They have good sound. My son uses them ALL then time.

title: Love it
text: Absolutely love mine! Use it in the car, cutting grass, work calls! Everywhere at all times!

title: Wow
text: These premium aftershock headphones are worth the difference. Charge port is 1000x times better than the base models, these where a gift to my wife. I will be switching to these as well.

title: Headset
text: This is the best headset I have ever owned. The sound quality is perfect and it is very easy to use. No one else can hear what is being said.

title: Berst headphones, very hi quality
text: Own far too many headphones sets but this is the best yet!

title: These are the bomb!
text: Where have these been all my life (since they were invented)?  They are not meant to noise canceling, obv.  They are comfortable and responsive to voice directions.  Long battery life.  My only gripe is that the volume is limited. Have you ever wanted to walk on the beach and listen to music AND the waves?  Now you can.

title: Great for using in many ways
text:
First, you have to take into account that these are tiny sealed headphones and are never going to give you the same performance as over the ear headphones. With that in mind, I believe you will enjoy these immensely.

What I use them for:

1. Outdoor activities (Hiking, Kayaking, etc) - this is so I can still hear nature (both friendly and not so friendly) and any fellow hikers. It's great to have a soundtrack that doesn't drown out everything while you are outdoors.
2. Watching TV - I sync them to my Apple TV and listen to TV when I'm doing things around the house and so I don't have to crank up the volume on my TV
3. Listening to podcasts/audiobooks on my iPhone - great way to get away from the stupid wired headphones
4. Working out at the gym - sync this to my Apple Watch and its a great way to have very little to carry around the gym

Pros -

1. No issues so far with heavy amounts of sweat while wearing them
2. Good ba

title: My new favorite toy (and they perform far better than a toy)
text:
Ultra thin. Nearly weightless, compatible with eyewear, forget I have them on. Sealed construction, protects against moisture. Kick-ass charger. Battery life is very long. Charging is fast. Clear podcasts and music. Bluetooth range is quite good. Crisp, clear microphone for calls - friends say I sound clearer and my callers sound more "present."

Hope this helps you shop!

title: Absolutely delighted
text: The device is well-made and well designed - lightweight, comfortable, sleek, intuitive.  The company uses top quality materials and has an iron-clad guarantee they really stand behind.  Customer service is a snap.  I'm so happy with this item and I think you will be too.

title: Nice Headphones
text:
Like: lightweight, easy to use, love magnetic hook up for charging

Dislike: the voice on unit-way too quiet and and her tone is way to low. Hard to hear if unit is connected.

title: Love this
text: I have the heavier 'model' of the bone conduction ...and keep going back to these due to comfort.  Wish they came in more colors!  Worth the extra $.

title: I’m happy with em so far! Would recommend them.
text: My first bone conductor headphones and I’m pleasantly surprised by the sound quality. Can hear everything around me and still hear the headphones clearly. If you’d like cancel out noise around you just pop in the ear plugs that come with it. Can comfortably lay on my side with earphones on, no ear discomfort. The fit is pretty good as well. Don’t have to worry about the popping out of my ear like some other more expensive ear buds.

title: A good recommendation from a friend
text:
I was not in the market for new earphones but a friend, whos opinion I value, had received some as a gift and raved about how much he enjoyed using these when riding his bike.

I bought a pair and really like them.

The blue tooth link seems to be a bit stronger than other earbuds I own.

title: Better than the other models
text: This is my second pair of aftershokz and I still love them I have the open ears and aeroplex and the aeroplex have better sound lighter and smaller I like the magnetic charging better too will buy again and for a gift as well. Highly recommend

title: My mom loves these
text: No more earbuds falling out of her ears and she said they sound amazing whether it’s the phone or music.

title: Beast earphones Ive owned.
text: I keep them on 8 hours a day.  Comfortable.  Great sound.  I use silicone plugs when I'm in the shop and the sound quality goes down but not too bad.

title: AMAZING headphones ever!!
text:
Update: I love mine so much I bought another pair for my sister. I absolutely love these headphones. I didn't think you could get good sound out of a device not stuck in your ears, but sure I was wrong!!
I have small ears and regular earbuds are too big and never, ever stay put in my ears, which means I drop them on the floor constantly which requires cleaning...big inconvenience. On top of that, while I like listening to music, books, podcasts I can't do it while walking because I can't stand not being able to hear what's going on around me. A friend told me about open ear AfterShokz and it took me a few months to finally take the plunge. I got them a few days ago with 60% battery... I listened music for ~4 hours before I got a low battery notification. I recharged them and I had them on every day since, so battery life seems good.
Other Pros:
They are very light, fit well and I can wear them for a few hours without being uncomfortable.
The sound is very cle

title: Comfortable to wear.
text: If driving long distance I like to lister to audiobooks, can hear the books as well as everything going on around you.  Also use at home every day.

title: Most comfortable headphones all day long
text: I bought these specifically for my all day long virtual learning sessions.  I produce sessions for corporate trainings and needed headphones I could comfortably wear all day.  I tried normal speakers (did not work well for me), I tried ear phones (hated the wire connections), I tried over the ear headphones (uncomfortable all day) and then I got these.  They are perfect for me.  I needed the 8 hour battery life for all day sessions so I paid a bit more for this model.  I use the speakers only (my mic is a separate table top mic) in my classes held in Zoom, Webex and Teams.  Sound is good (used use for talking sound only, not music).  Comfort is the best of all methods I have tried.  Noise cancellation is not applicable since these sit on the jawbone and not over the ear--so the ear is open and can hear all background noise (although one can insert supplied foam ear plugs to block outside noise and still hear sound from these headphones).  My only recommended improvement would be somewa

title: they work perfectly
text: Bone conduction does not give you the best sound quality! But, open ears is better when cycling and just plain comfort. I have worn these all day, 12 hours and no pain. I wear a lot of headsets, earplugs, and these are my go to for most activities. If you like to hear your environment, there is nothing better.

title: Make sure you know what you are getting before buying these.
text: These are bone induction headphones. They do not sound like my beats studio, or like my power beats pro. The reason for that is because of the first sentence I wrote. These are not the headphones for everyone, but these are the headphones I was looking for. If I want to go out on a run, I want to be able to hear music, but I also need to be able to hear my surroundings. That is the reason I bought these. I want to be aware of my surroundings, but also listen to my music. I also wanted to be able to listen to my music without it bothering others around me. If that is what you need, these are the ones that meets your needs. The music quality takes a hit because of the way it is being delivered to you, but in my opinion isn't enough to warrant me throwing them out. I hope this helps anyone who is unsure about these.

title: Perfect for outdoors where you need to hear what’s going on
text: These are awesome, you are able to hear the outside world while listening to clear crisp music.

title: Best ear phones with bone conduction
text: Like the fit and rides well under my sick hat! Great to hear clearly when spoken to. Ear canal is clear except when I want the full effect and don't need to worry about surroundings. WOW!  Hate to admit my son turned me on to these. Easy setup and registration. Controls pretty simple and forgiving. Like the voice notifications. Made and recieved call flawlessly and clear as a bell. Best no nagging ear irritation, waxy ear buds or losing another ear piece. Can't say enough and price is great!

title: They work and are comfortable
text: I have a major problem withy sweaty, itchy ears after long use of headphones. These avoid that completely and sound much better than I had expected. They work well during most exercise and also for general use.

title: Doctor wears in hospital
text: Comfortable to wear continuously all day in the hospital, used for phone calls throughout the day.  Battery lasts for the whole shift. Nice because I can leave them on and still hear patients/ colleagues talking.

title: My 2nd pair!!!
text: Absolutely LOVE these head phones!! I cant have anything plug INTO my ears...now i have the perfect thing, cant come off, not problems, loud and clear...I can wear them at work on the farm and never miss a thing. Friends say sounds like I'm holding the phone when talking. Very clear both ways.

title: Ears - no pain
text: First time I used them it was 89% humidity and high temps they didn't side out when I bent down.  I didn't have to the volume up and could listen to a book, while cutting grass.  Best of all my inner ear didn't hurt.  Looking forward to many years of use.

title: Not for me
text: I could feel the music vibrating through my jaw.  It tickled.  The sound was fine. It worked fine.  It was me.  So some folks just can't use this style.

title: Not durable at all AND the charging cable is badly designed
text:
I love bone conduction headphones. I had 2 pairs of the discontinued Trekz Titanium model, but they kept breaking where the band met the control unit on the earpiece. So, lured in by the superior sound and what seemed to be a more durable design, I saved up and bought some. I really loved them! They sound great, they're comfortable, they're incredibly light...

BUT GUESS WHAT

Breakage at the same spot as the others. I don't sleep in them or lay on them or anything. I generally take good care of stuff. I've had them almost a year without much visible wear. My kid crashed into me the other day, and I heard a pop. Same spot as my other ones. I'm done with this brand for now. Maybe their next generation will be more durable.

Oh, and the charging cable design is garbage. The connection isn't solid, and the magnets in the cable mean I can't just leave my cable plugged in. Once, a cable fell into a cat water dish and did some electrochemistry. Another time it t

title: Worth the extra expense.
text:
I’ve bought 3 cheaper BCH & retuned them all. I finally broke down & spent the cash on these.
Worth it!

title: It’s nice.
text: I like the headphones, but it seems that I can only wear them if I am sitting up or standing. When I am lying down on the bed to watch a movie, it is not comfortable. I still cannot figure out why zoom does not work through these headphones, all the others do.

title: First impression
text: The sound is more crisp then the less expensive version. I hope they last...

title: Brilliant concept.
text: I really like this headset. The open ear concept is revolutionary. It allows me to hear the ambient sounds while still providing engaging music through the headset bone conduction technology. There is absolutely no movement of the headset while running, and it doesn’t interfere with glasses or a cap.

title: No complaints on my 3 sets!
text: I bought 3 sets for Christmas gifts and everyone loves them! The range my husband gets away from his phone is quite amazing. So I bought my son in law and brother in law a set and no complaints!!

title: Great for doing things around the house and still being able to hear
text: Love these headphones!!  I wear them all over the house.  Very handy.  The sound quality won't blow you away but I enjoy them for how convenient and oh so comfortable they are.  Put in ear plugs and you can cut the grass with them.  It's really cool how they work.  Also has a nice pause button that is easy to use.  So nice I bought a second pair for the spouse.

title: Love them!
text: You can still hear the world around you while you are listening to music, audiobooks, or meditations. I was not sure I would like them but found they are quite comfortable as you have nothing directly in your ears and they sit comfortably beside your ears. I have worn them hours at a time without feeling tired or irritated from them. Great while exercising and doing daily activities. I have not used the ear plugs that came with them so I can't say one way or another about them. The point for me to order them was that I would not have something directly in my ear and I could still hear the world around me. The charge lasts a long time. It tells you the battery life left when you turn them on. I have used them several times while on a conference call and have been happy with the sound quality over all.

title: Loving it.
text: Super comfortable.  I can keep it on my head all day and don’t even realize it is there.  To get a good base you need to use year plugs.  I was not concerned about listening to music anyway.  I wanted to be hands free for phone calls.  It does a great work.  My husband purchased one after trying mine.  He hates ear phones.  But he loved this one because it doesn’t bother him while using it.  I hope it last long enough to pay it self off.  Recommend!

title: Great sound and light on your ears
text: I have a different set of bone conduction earphones and these are soooooo much better. I didn’t realise the poor quality of the others until I tried these. The only negative for me is that they have a different/new  charge wire. This means you can only charge with this wire. Another wire to hang off my outlet.

title: Old ears - Love it!
text:
Preliminary review because I haven't had them long enough to tell about wearing it for a few hours. These are much easier on the ears then the Titanium version. They sound great for ears that no longer hear the higher frequencies which usually makes some music sound awful.

All my earbuds and headphones are pretty much being mothballed.

title: Great For Construction Work.
text: I use these for work as I work in construction, they sound great and I can still hear people around me. The battery lasts a long time as well. I also like that they fit snug yet not tight on my head.
